page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, supported by a cache hierarchy of 64 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scoring in the average tier. However, the 48-core parallel capacity delivers strong multi-threaded performance. The Passmark CPU score, extended instructions test, encryption test, and physics test all rank in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also registers a strong result at 341.35 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 3013.1 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 62.7 GB/sec.

The shared CPU allocation model and lower single-core clock speed present a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, high-frequency single-thread execution. For instance, medium-sized LLM text generation is slow, performing in the bottom 10% at 6.23 tokens/sec. Conversely, the high memory capacity and strong multi-core throughput offer qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense, memory-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated local NVMe storage. This server is well-suited for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data processing, memory-intensive web serving, and parallelized software compilation.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is a memory-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor, it features 48 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU resources. Benchmark data indicates a tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 10% for stress-ng, whereas multi-core capabilities are strong, with Passmark CPU and encryption scores in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth remains average, with uncached large block reads at 127.36 GB/sec. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive, parallelized workloads such as database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and software compilation, where high core counts and large memory footprints are prioritized over single-thread speed.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 KVM virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores and 384 GB of RAM. It offers strong multi-core performance and high memory density for parallelized database workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is a KVM virtual server with 48 AMD EPYC cores and 384 GB RAM, optimized for memory-heavy parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual server featuring forty-eight cores and substantial RAM for demanding multi-threaded database and processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC 7542 physical cores with shared CPU allocation
384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% on Passmark CPU tests
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and parallel data processing
